frame_analyses.description: Initial view showing Penrose drain with suture passing through both marks. Needle holder visible with suture loaded.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to be positioned through both marks on the drain

frame_analyses.description: Continued suture placement phase with instruments manipulating suture through the drain marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both marks appear to have suture passing through them

frame_analyses.description: Suture placement completion, preparing to begin knot tying sequence.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture positioned appropriately through both drain marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot. Creating double wrap for the first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be forming surgeon's knot with double throw technique

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw with double wrap. Right hand dominant in this throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot first throw being secured properly

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw, switching to left hand dominance as required.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed between first and second throws

frame_analyses.description: Completing second throw with single wrap, left hand dominant.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw completed with proper hand switch

frame_analyses.description: Beginning third throw, switching back to right hand dominance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed between second and third throws

frame_analyses.description: Preparing to cut suture ends. Both throws completed and knot appears secure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears well-formed and ready for cutting

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed knot with instruments withdrawn. Task completion achieved.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends appear cut, task completed

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw completed
Single throw with proper hand switch from throw 1
Single throw with proper hand switch from throw 2

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gap after knot tightening

technique_summary: Good execution of intracorporeal suturing with proper surgeon's knot and hand switching technique. Knot appears secure and drain closure is adequate.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to reduce overall completion time
Focus on more efficient instrument movements during knot tying phases
